title: Alma's Conversion
description: The most celebrated chiasmus in the Book of Mormon. Alma recounts his conversion to his son Helaman, with 17 mirrored elements centering on Jesus Christ as the turning point.

levels.label: A
levels.text: Give ear to my words
levels.matchText: According to his word

levels.label: B
levels.text: Keep commandments and ye shall prosper in the land
levels.matchText: Inasmuch as ye keep the commandments ye shall prosper in the land

levels.label: C
levels.text: Do as I have done
levels.matchText: That which our fathers have said is true

levels.label: D
levels.text: Remember the captivity of our fathers
levels.matchText: He has brought our fathers out of bondage and captivity

levels.label: E
levels.text: They were in bondage
levels.matchText: Retained in bondage

levels.label: F
levels.text: He surely did deliver them
levels.matchText: Knowledge of God delivered them

levels.label: G
levels.text: Trust in God
levels.matchText: Put their trust in God

levels.label: H
levels.text: Supported in trials, troubles, and afflictions
levels.matchText: Supported under trials, troubles, afflictions

levels.label: I
levels.text: Lifted up at the last day
levels.matchText: Raised up at the last day

levels.label: J
levels.text: I know this not of myself but of God
levels.matchText: Not of any temporal thing but of God

levels.label: K
levels.text: Born of God
levels.matchText: Born of God

levels.label: L
levels.text: Sought to destroy the church
levels.matchText: Labored to bring souls to repentance

levels.label: M
levels.text: My limbs were paralyzed
levels.matchText: I stood upon my feet / my limbs received their strength

levels.label: N
levels.text: Fear of being in the presence of God
levels.matchText: Desire to be in the presence of God

levels.label: O
levels.text: Pains of a damned soul
levels.matchText: Exquisite joy

levels.label: P
levels.text: Harrowed up by the memory of sins
levels.matchText: Harrowed up by the memory of sins no more

levels.label: Q
levels.text: I remembered Jesus Christ, a Son of God — I cried: Jesus, thou Son of God, have mercy on me

centerText: Jesus Christ, Son of God — Atone, mind, heart

title: Becoming as a Child through the Atonement
description: King Benjamin's speech contains this tightly constructed chiasmus about the natural man and the atonement. Statistically validated as intentional.
source: Welch, 'Chiasmus in the Book of Mormon' (1969); Statistical validation by Boyd & Hilton

levels.label: A
levels.text: Humble themselves
levels.matchText: Submissive, meek, humble

levels.label: B
levels.text: Become as little children
levels.matchText: Becomes as a child

levels.label: C
levels.text: Believe in the atoning blood of Christ
levels.matchText: Becomes a saint through the atonement

levels.label: D
levels.text: The natural man
levels.matchText: Puts off the natural man

levels.label: E
levels.text: Is an enemy to God
levels.matchText: Yields to the enticings of the Holy Spirit

levels.label: F
levels.text: Has been from the fall of Adam

centerText: Has been from the fall of Adam, and will be forever and ever

title: Taking the Name of Christ
description: The first chiasmus Welch discovered in the Book of Mormon (1967). King Benjamin urges his people to take upon them the name of Christ. Statistically one of the strongest chiasms in the Book of Mormon.
source: Welch, 'Chiasmus in the Book of Mormon'; BYU Studies chart 131

levels.label: A
levels.text: Not willing to take upon the name of Christ
levels.matchText: The name by which he shall call you

levels.label: B
levels.text: Called by some other name
levels.matchText: You shall hear and know the voice by which you shall be called

levels.label: C
levels.text: Found on the left hand of God
levels.matchText: Not found on the left hand of God

levels.label: D
levels.text: Remember to retain the name always written in your hearts
levels.matchText: Remember to retain the name

levels.label: E
levels.text: Never blotted out
levels.matchText: Not blotted out except through transgression

levels.label: F
levels.text: Except it be through transgression

centerText: Take heed that ye do not transgress

title: The Sixty-Fourth Year of Peace
description: A summary of the sixty-fourth year of the reign of judges, structured as a complete chiasm. Statistically validated as intentional. The Hebrew elements in the center (Lord/Zedekiah both containing Yahweh) suggest this worked better in the original language.
source: Welch; Meridian Magazine '10 Best Chiasms in the Book of Mormon'

levels.label: A
levels.text: Peace in all the land
levels.matchText: Peace in all the land

levels.label: B
levels.text: Free travel and trade between Nephites and Lamanites
levels.matchText: Great prosperity, free travel

levels.label: C
levels.text: Became exceedingly rich
levels.matchText: Became rich

levels.label: D
levels.text: Abundance of gold, silver, precious metals
levels.matchText: Precious ores, metals, riches

levels.label: E
levels.text: Land south called Lehi
levels.matchText: Land north called Mulek

levels.label: F
levels.text: Lehi — son of Zedekiah
levels.matchText: Mulek — son of Zedekiah

levels.label: G
levels.text: The Lord brought them

centerText: The Lord brought Mulek into the land north, and Lehi into the land south

title: The Law of Restoration
description: Alma teaches his son about the law of restoration using a chiastic list of paired virtues: good, righteous, just, and merciful — first as rewards, then reversed as conditions.
source: Welch; Jeff Lindsay, 'Chiasmus in the Book of Mormon'

levels.label: A
levels.text: Good for that which is good
levels.matchText: Do good continually / good rewarded unto you

levels.label: B
levels.text: Righteous for that which is righteous
levels.matchText: Judge righteously / righteous judgment restored

levels.label: C
levels.text: Just for that which is just
levels.matchText: Deal justly / justice restored

levels.label: D
levels.text: Merciful for that which is merciful
levels.matchText: Be merciful unto your brethren / mercy restored

centerText: Mercy — the law of restoration restores what you give

title: The Great and Last Sacrifice
description: Amulek's teaching on the necessity of the atonement forms a tight single-verse chiasmus.
source: Jeff Lindsay, 'Chiasmus in the Book of Mormon'

levels.label: A
levels.text: An atonement should be made
levels.matchText: Which atonement should be made

levels.label: B
levels.text: According to the great plan of the Eternal God
levels.matchText: Except it be through the atonement

levels.label: C
levels.text: There must be an atonement made
levels.matchText: All mankind must unavoidably perish

levels.label: D
levels.text: Or else all mankind must unavoidably perish

centerText: All mankind must perish — all are hardened, fallen, and lost

title: O That I Were an Angel
description: Alma's poetic reflections form a unique A-B-C-C-C-B-A chiasmus with a triple repetition at the center rather than a single pivot, paralleling structures found in ancient Mesoamerican inscriptions.
source: Meridian Magazine; parallels with Stela C at Quiriguá

levels.label: A
levels.text: O that I were an angel and could have the wish of mine heart
levels.matchText: Why should I desire more than to perform the work to which I have been called?

levels.label: B
levels.text: Go forth and speak with the trump of God
levels.matchText: The Lord grants unto all nations their own teachers

levels.label: C
levels.text: Shake the earth / cry repentance unto every people
levels.matchText: I ought to be content with the things allotted unto me

levels.label: D
levels.text: That there were no more sorrow upon the face of the earth (triple center pattern)

centerText: Joy or remorse according to desire — three parallel expressions of consequence

title: Christ's Covenant Teachings
description: Christ's teachings about the Father's covenant with Israel span two chapters with approximately 20 mirrored elements.
source: Jeff Lindsay, 'Chiasmus in the Book of Mormon'

levels.label: A
levels.text: Father and Son work together
levels.matchText: Father and Son work together

levels.label: B
levels.text: Remnant gathered from among all nations
levels.matchText: Remnant gathered by gentiles

levels.label: C
levels.text: Covenant land inheritance
levels.matchText: New Jerusalem established

levels.label: D
levels.text: Gospel preached, Zion established
levels.matchText: Great and marvelous work, marred servant

levels.label: E
levels.text: Kings shall shut their mouths

centerText: That which had not been told them shall they see; that which they had not heard shall they consider

title: The Genesis Flood Narrative
description: One of the most famous and widely-recognized chiasms in all of literature. The entire flood narrative is structured as a grand chiasm centering on 'God remembered Noah.' Identified by Gordon Wenham (1978) and Yehuda Radday.
source: Wenham, 'The Coherence of the Flood Narrative,' Vetus Testamentum 28 (1978): 336-48

levels.label: A
levels.text: Violence and corruption in creation
levels.matchText: Covenant blessing for new creation

levels.label: B
levels.text: First divine speech: destruction decreed
levels.matchText: God's promise to preserve order

levels.label: C
levels.text: Second divine speech: enter the ark
levels.matchText: Third divine speech: leave the ark

levels.label: D
levels.text: Flood begins — rain 40 days
levels.matchText: Earth dries — wait periods

levels.label: E
levels.text: Waters rise — 150 days
levels.matchText: Waters recede — 150 days

levels.label: F
levels.text: God remembered Noah

centerText: God remembered Noah — divine mercy at the center of judgment

title: The Fall of Adam and Eve
description: The narrative of the Fall is arranged as a multi-level chiasm, centering on the serpent's curse — the point where God begins pronouncing consequences.

levels.label: A
levels.text: You will be like God
levels.matchText: Man has become like one of us

levels.label: B
levels.text: Fig leaf coverings (human-made)
levels.matchText: God provides garments of skin

levels.label: C
levels.text: The woman (unnamed)
levels.matchText: The woman named Eve

levels.label: D
levels.text: God questions Adam: Where are you?
levels.matchText: Adam's curse: toil, dust, death

levels.label: E
levels.text: Eve blamed / Serpent blamed
levels.matchText: Eve's consequences: pain, subjection

levels.label: F
levels.text: The serpent is cursed

centerText: The serpent cursed — enmity between seed of woman and serpent (protoevangelium)

title: Blood Retribution
description: A single-verse chiasmus demonstrating reciprocal justice in its most compact form.

levels.label: A
levels.text: Whoever sheds
levels.matchText: Shall be shed

levels.label: B
levels.text: The blood
levels.matchText: His blood

levels.label: C
levels.text: Of man
levels.matchText: By man

centerText: Man — both victim and instrument of justice

title: Lex Talionis — Eye for Eye
description: The famous 'eye for eye' law of proportional justice is arranged as a nine-part chiasmus with nested internal chiasms. First identified by Boys in 1825.

levels.label: A
levels.text: Whoever takes the life of any human being shall be put to death
levels.matchText: Whoever kills a man shall be put to death

levels.label: B
levels.text: Whoever takes the life of an animal shall make restitution
levels.matchText: Whoever kills an animal shall make restitution

levels.label: C
levels.text: Whoever injures his neighbor
levels.matchText: As he has injured a man

levels.label: D
levels.text: Fracture for fracture
levels.matchText: Tooth for tooth

levels.label: E
levels.text: Eye for eye

centerText: Eye for eye — the principle of proportional justice

title: The Song of Moses
description: Moses' final song to Israel before his death is structured as a grand chiasm, with God's wrath as the central turning point, framed by descriptions of faithfulness and the song's opening/closing formula.

levels.label: A
levels.text: The words of this song
levels.matchText: The words of this song

levels.label: B
levels.text: A faithful God, without deceit — He is the Rock
levels.matchText: There is no god besides Me — I kill and make alive

levels.label: C
levels.text: You forgot the God who gave you birth
levels.matchText: These foes would mistakenly boast — their vine is of Sodom

levels.label: D
levels.text: The wrath of God provoked by Israel's unfaithfulness

centerText: God's wrath — I will heap misfortunes on them; I will spend my arrows on them

title: God's Blessing Among All Nations
description: A compact and elegant chiasmus moving from divine blessing to universal praise, centering on God's righteous judgment of all peoples.

levels.label: A
levels.text: God be gracious to us and bless us
levels.matchText: God, our God, blesses us — all the ends of the earth shall fear him

levels.label: B
levels.text: Let the peoples praise thee, O God; let all the peoples praise thee
levels.matchText: Let the peoples praise thee, O God; let all the peoples praise thee

levels.label: C
levels.text: Let the nations be glad and sing for joy
levels.matchText: Thou dost guide the nations on the earth

levels.label: D
levels.text: For thou wilt judge the peoples with uprightness

centerText: God judges the peoples with uprightness

title: Come, Buy Without Money
description: Isaiah's great invitation chapter is structured as a chiasmus centering on the call to repentance, framed by promises of sustenance and the power of God's word.

levels.label: A
levels.text: Come, buy food and drink without money — hear and your soul shall live
levels.matchText: Rain and snow water the earth, giving seed and bread — God's word accomplishes its purpose

levels.label: B
levels.text: Everlasting covenant, sure mercies of David — nations called to witness
levels.matchText: God's thoughts and ways higher than man's — as heavens higher than earth

levels.label: C
levels.text: Seek the Lord while he may be found — forsake wicked ways — God will have mercy and abundantly pardon

centerText: Seek the Lord while he may be found — the wicked shall forsake his way and return unto the Lord

title: Woe Unto Them — Evil and Good Reversed
description: A single verse containing three compact chiasms in sequence: evil/good, darkness/light, bitter/sweet — each reversing moral categories.

levels.label: A
levels.text: Call evil good
levels.matchText: And good evil

levels.label: B
levels.text: Put darkness for light
levels.matchText: And light for darkness

levels.label: C
levels.text: Put bitter for sweet
levels.matchText: And sweet for bitter

centerText: Three parallel chiasms showing the complete inversion of moral order

title: Seven Things the Lord Hates
description: The seven abominations form a chiastic structure centering on 'a heart that devises wicked plans' — pride, deception, and violence originate from a corrupt heart.

levels.label: A
levels.text: Haughty eyes (pride)
levels.matchText: One who spreads strife among brothers (social destruction)

levels.label: B
levels.text: A lying tongue (deception by word)
levels.matchText: A false witness who utters lies (deception by testimony)

levels.label: C
levels.text: Hands that shed innocent blood (violence)
levels.matchText: Feet that run rapidly to evil (rushing toward violence)

levels.label: D
levels.text: A heart that devises wicked plans

centerText: A heart that devises wicked plans — the corrupt heart as source of all evil

title: Daniel's Aramaic Section — Four Kingdoms
description: The entire Aramaic section of Daniel (chapters 2-7) forms a grand chiasm. The outer pair (ch. 2 and 7) concern four world kingdoms replaced by God's kingdom; the middle pair (ch. 3 and 6) tell of miraculous deliverance; the inner pair (ch. 4 and 5) show God humbling pagan kings.
source: Biblical Chiasm Exchange; widely recognized in scholarship

levels.label: A
levels.text: A dream of four kingdoms replaced by God's kingdom (Nebuchadnezzar's statue)
levels.matchText: A vision of four kingdoms replaced by God's kingdom (four beasts)

levels.label: B
levels.text: Three friends delivered from the fiery furnace
levels.matchText: Daniel delivered from the lions' den

levels.label: C
levels.text: God humbles Nebuchadnezzar (dream interpreted by Daniel)
levels.matchText: God humbles Belshazzar (handwriting interpreted by Daniel)

centerText: God's sovereignty over pagan rulers — pride brought low

title: Nebuchadnezzar's Dream — Internal Structure
description: Within Daniel 2 itself, the narrative follows a chiastic pattern centering on Daniel's prayer and God's revelation — only God can reveal the future.

levels.label: A
levels.text: Nebuchadnezzar has a dream and is troubled
levels.matchText: Nebuchadnezzar recognizes Daniel's God and elevates Daniel

levels.label: B
levels.text: Nebuchadnezzar demands the Chaldeans reveal dream
levels.matchText: Daniel reveals the dream and interpretation

levels.label: C
levels.text: Chaldeans confess: no one can tell the dream except the gods
levels.matchText: Daniel declares: there is a God in heaven who reveals mysteries

levels.label: D
levels.text: Daniel speaks to Arioch
levels.matchText: Daniel speaks to Arioch

levels.label: E
levels.text: Daniel receives the mystery in a night vision — praises God who controls the future

centerText: God reveals mysteries — He changes times and seasons, removes and sets up kings

title: The Prologue — The Word Made Flesh
description: The prologue of John's Gospel is one of the most widely recognized chiasms in the New Testament. The structure moves from the cosmic Word to the intimate gift of becoming children of God at its center.

levels.label: A
levels.text: The Word was God — with God in the beginning
levels.matchText: The only-begotten Son has declared the Father

levels.label: B
levels.text: All things were made through him
levels.matchText: Grace and truth came through Jesus Christ

levels.label: C
levels.text: In him was life; the life was the light of men — light shines in darkness
levels.matchText: Of his fullness we have received, grace for grace

levels.label: D
levels.text: John came as a witness to the Light
levels.matchText: John bore witness: He who comes after me ranks before me

levels.label: E
levels.text: He was in the world, the world knew him not
levels.matchText: The Word became flesh and dwelt among us — we beheld his glory

levels.label: F
levels.text: He came to his own, and his own received him not
levels.matchText: Born not of blood nor of the will of the flesh but of God

levels.label: G
levels.text: To as many as received him — power to become children of God

centerText: Power to become the children of God — to those who believe on his name

title: The Sermon on the Mount
description: The entire Sermon on the Mount is structured as a chiasm with the Lord's Prayer at its theological center. The framework moves from setting to beatitudes, through the law and righteous practice, centering on prayer.
discoveredBy: Dale Allison; C. H. Lohr; others

levels.label: A
levels.text: Frame: Jesus sees crowds, goes up mountain, sits, disciples come
levels.matchText: Frame: Crowds astonished at his teaching, he comes down

levels.label: B
levels.text: Beatitudes and warnings about persecution — salt and light
levels.matchText: Warnings with contrasting parables — wise and foolish builders

levels.label: C
levels.text: Fulfilling the Law and Prophets — not one jot shall pass
levels.matchText: The Golden Rule — this is the Law and the Prophets

levels.label: D
levels.text: Six antitheses: You have heard it said... but I say
levels.matchText: Possessions, judging, and asking — treasure, motes, pearls

levels.label: E
levels.text: Righteousness before God: almsgiving
levels.matchText: Righteousness before God: fasting

levels.label: F
levels.text: Prayer instruction: avoid vain repetitions
levels.matchText: Prayer conclusion: if you forgive, your Father will forgive

levels.label: G
levels.text: The Lord's Prayer

centerText: The Lord's Prayer — Our Father which art in heaven, hallowed be thy name

title: The Sabbath Was Made for Man
description: A compact single-verse chiasmus where Jesus reverses the expected relationship between man and Sabbath.

levels.label: A
levels.text: The sabbath was made
levels.matchText: Not man for the sabbath

levels.label: B
levels.text: For man
levels.matchText: Man

centerText: Purpose relationship inverted: human dignity takes priority over religious observance

title: Exalted and Humbled
description: A compact chiasmus where Jesus states the principle of divine reversal.

levels.label: A
levels.text: Whoever exalts himself
levels.matchText: Shall be exalted

levels.label: B
levels.text: Shall be humbled
levels.matchText: Whoever humbles himself

centerText: Divine reversal: God opposes the proud and lifts the humble

title: Reconciliation Through Christ
description: Paul's teaching on Jew-Gentile reconciliation through Christ is structured as a chiasm centering on Christ breaking down the dividing wall.

levels.label: A
levels.text: Without Christ — alienated from Israel
levels.matchText: No longer strangers — fellow citizens of God's household

levels.label: B
levels.text: Having no hope, without God in the world
levels.matchText: Access to the Father in one Spirit

levels.label: C
levels.text: But now in Christ Jesus, those far off brought near by his blood
levels.matchText: He came and preached peace to those far and near

levels.label: D
levels.text: He is our peace — made both groups one — broke down the dividing wall of hostility

centerText: Christ is our peace — He broke down the dividing wall between Jew and Gentile

title: The Christ Hymn — Kenosis
description: The great kenosis hymn traces Christ's descent from divine equality to death on a cross, then his exaltation — a V-shaped chiastic descent and ascent.
source: Marg Mowczko; Tyndale Bulletin; multiple commentaries

levels.label: A
levels.text: Being in the form of God — equality with God
levels.matchText: God highly exalted him — gave him the name above every name

levels.label: B
levels.text: Emptied himself, taking the form of a servant
levels.matchText: Every knee should bow — in heaven, on earth, under the earth

levels.label: C
levels.text: Made in the likeness of men / found in appearance as a man
levels.matchText: Every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord

levels.label: D
levels.text: Humbled himself, became obedient unto death — even death on a cross

centerText: Death on a cross — the lowest point of Christ's descent becomes the pivot to exaltation

title: Born Again by the Word of God
description: Peter's teaching on spiritual rebirth contrasts the eternal Word of God with the transience of human glory using grass and flower imagery.

levels.label: A
levels.text: Born again by the word of God
levels.matchText: This is the word which by the gospel is preached unto you

levels.label: B
levels.text: Which liveth and abideth for ever
levels.matchText: The word of the Lord endureth for ever

levels.label: C
levels.text: All flesh is as grass
levels.matchText: The grass withereth

levels.label: D
levels.text: The glory of man as the flower of grass
levels.matchText: And the flower thereof falleth away

centerText: Human glory is temporary like grass and flowers — only God's word endures

title: The First Shall Be Last
description: The parable of the workers in the vineyard is bounded by a classic chiastic saying — 'the first shall be last and the last first' — which then reverses at the close.

levels.label: A
levels.text: The first shall be last
levels.matchText: The last shall be first

levels.label: B
levels.text: And the last first
levels.matchText: And the first last

centerText: The parable of the laborers illustrates the reversal — God's grace defies human expectations of merit

title: Worship in Spirit and Truth
description: Jesus' teaching to the Samaritan woman about true worship forms a chiasmus centering on the nature of God as Spirit.

levels.label: A
levels.text: True worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and truth
levels.matchText: They that worship him must worship in spirit and truth

levels.label: B
levels.text: The Father seeketh such to worship him
levels.matchText: Worship him

levels.label: C
levels.text: God is a Spirit

centerText: God is a Spirit — His nature determines how He must be worshipped

title: Moses' Vision and Satan's Temptation
description: The entire first chapter of Moses in the Pearl of Great Price forms a concentric chiasmus. The central turning point is Moses calling upon God and receiving strength to resist Satan.
discoveredBy: Pearl of Great Price Central scholars
source: Pearl of Great Price Central, 'Chiasmus in Moses 1'

levels.label: A
levels.text: God speaks to Moses on an exceeding high mountain
levels.matchText: God speaks to Moses — writes these words (commission renewed)

levels.label: B
levels.text: God's glory and endless name revealed
levels.matchText: God's works and glory revealed again

levels.label: C
levels.text: God's work and glory: immortality and eternal life
levels.matchText: Moses sees every particle of the earth — God's creations

levels.label: D
levels.text: Moses beholds the world and the ends thereof
levels.matchText: Moses sees the earth again, every soul upon it

levels.label: E
levels.text: God's presence withdraws — man is nothing
levels.matchText: Satan departs — Moses left alone

levels.label: F
levels.text: Satan comes tempting: Worship me, Son of Man
levels.matchText: Moses commands Satan to depart in the name of the Only Begotten

levels.label: G
levels.text: Moses called upon God and received strength

centerText: Moses called upon God — received strength to resist Satan

title: Selection of Noble Rulers
description: Abraham's vision of the pre-mortal council contains a chiasmus centering on God standing among the spirits and declaring them rulers.
discoveredBy: Pearl of Great Price Central scholars
source: Pearl of Great Price Central, 'Chiasmus in the Book of Abraham'; BYU Studies

levels.label: A
levels.text: The Lord showed Abraham the intelligences organized before the world
levels.matchText: Thou wast chosen before thou wast born

levels.label: B
levels.text: Among all these were many noble and great ones
levels.matchText: Said unto Abraham: Thou art one of them

levels.label: C
levels.text: God saw these souls, that they were good
levels.matchText: He saw that they were good

levels.label: D
levels.text: He stood in the midst of them
levels.matchText: He stood among those that were spirits

levels.label: E
levels.text: These I will make my rulers

centerText: These I will make my rulers — divine election at the center

title: Abraham's Right to Priesthood
description: Abraham's declaration of his priestly lineage forms a compact chiasmus tracing the priesthood from himself back through the fathers to the beginning and back again.
discoveredBy: Pearl of Great Price Central scholars
source: Pearl of Great Price Central, 'Chiasmus in the Book of Abraham'

levels.label: A
levels.text: Conferred upon me
levels.matchText: Unto me

levels.label: B
levels.text: From the fathers
levels.matchText: Through the fathers

levels.label: C
levels.text: It came down from the fathers, from the beginning of time
levels.matchText: Down from the present time to the beginning — even from Adam

levels.label: D
levels.text: From before the foundation of the earth

centerText: Before the foundation of the earth — priesthood has pre-mortal origins

title: The Law by Which All Things Are Governed
description: The Olive Leaf revelation contains a chiasmus about kingdoms of glory and the law that governs them. While D&C chiasms are debated (since Joseph Smith dictated these revelations in English), this is the strongest statistical candidate.

levels.label: A
levels.text: That which is governed by law is preserved by law
levels.matchText: All kingdoms have a law given

levels.label: B
levels.text: That which is not governed by law cannot be preserved
levels.matchText: That which has no law — as if there were no creation

levels.label: C
levels.text: That which breaketh a law and abideth not by law
levels.matchText: Intelligence cleaveth unto intelligence

levels.label: D
levels.text: Cannot be sanctified by law, neither by mercy, justice, nor judgment

centerText: Cannot be sanctified by law, mercy, justice, or judgment — the consequences of lawlessness

title: Seven Woes to the Scribes and Pharisees
description: The seven woes Jesus pronounces have internal chiastic structure. The central three woes (3rd, 4th, 5th) are highly chiastic with repeated 'blind guides/blind men/blind' motifs, while the outer woes deal with exclusion and tombs.

levels.label: A
levels.text: Woe 1: Shut the kingdom of heaven — neither enter nor let others in
levels.matchText: Woe 7: Fill up the measure of your fathers — blood of the prophets

levels.label: B
levels.text: Woe 2: Devour widows' houses with long prayers
levels.matchText: Woe 6: Whitewashed tombs — outwardly righteous, inwardly dead

levels.label: C
levels.text: Woe 3: Make proselytes twice the children of hell — blind guides (temple/altar oaths)
levels.matchText: Woe 5: Clean outside of cup, inside full of extortion — blind Pharisee

levels.label: D
levels.text: Woe 4: Tithe mint, dill, cummin — neglect justice, mercy, faithfulness

centerText: Neglecting justice, mercy, and faithfulness — the weightier matters of the law

title: God's Thoughts Higher Than Man's
description: A tightly constructed two-verse chiasmus comparing God's thoughts/ways with human thoughts/ways, centering on 'as the heavens are higher than the earth.'

levels.label: A
levels.text: My thoughts are not your thoughts
levels.matchText: So are my thoughts higher than your thoughts

levels.label: B
levels.text: Neither are your ways my ways
levels.matchText: So are my ways higher than your ways

levels.label: C
levels.text: As the heavens are higher than the earth

centerText: As the heavens are higher than the earth — infinite gap between divine and human perspective

title: Nephi's Faith Declaration
description: Nephi's rebuke of his brothers contains chiastic elements centering on God's dealings with Israel.

levels.label: A
levels.text: The Lord has created the earth that it should be inhabited
levels.matchText: The Lord esteems all flesh in one — the righteous are favored

levels.label: B
levels.text: He raised up a righteous nation and destroyed the wicked
levels.matchText: Do ye believe our fathers would have been led from Jerusalem?

levels.label: C
levels.text: Led the children of Israel out of Egypt
levels.matchText: He led them through the wilderness

levels.label: D
levels.text: Moses commanded: look and live / bitten by serpents

centerText: The brazen serpent — a type of Christ, requiring faith to be healed

title: A Bible, A Bible
description: The Lord's declaration about multiple scriptural witnesses may contain chiastic elements, though the pattern is looser and debated among scholars.

levels.label: A
levels.text: A Bible! A Bible! We have got a Bible
levels.matchText: The words of the Jews and of the Nephites shall be gathered in one

levels.label: B
levels.text: Many of the Gentiles shall say
levels.matchText: I speak unto all the nations of the earth

levels.label: C
levels.text: I shall bring forth more of my word
levels.matchText: I commanded the Nephites to write

levels.label: D
levels.text: Know ye not that there are more nations than one?

centerText: God speaks to all nations — not limited to one people or one book

title: Enos' Prayer
description: Enos' prayer narrative may exhibit loose chiastic tendencies as his concern expands from self to Nephites to Lamanites, then the pattern reverses. The structure is debated.

levels.label: A
levels.text: Enos wrestles before God — his own sins
levels.matchText: Faith in God to preserve records

levels.label: B
levels.text: Prays for the Nephites — God covenants
levels.matchText: Prays for records to be preserved for Lamanites

levels.label: C
levels.text: Prays for the Lamanites

centerText: Expanding circle of concern reaches the Lamanites

title: The Book of Mosiah — Grand Structure
description: The entire book of Mosiah may be organized as a large-scale chiasm centered on Abinadi's prophecies about Christ.
source: Jeff Lindsay, 'Chiasmus in the Book of Mormon'

levels.label: A
levels.text: King Benjamin exhorts his sons
levels.matchText: Mosiah exhorts the people

levels.label: B
levels.text: Benjamin's speech — angel's words
levels.matchText: Alma's words — angel's words to Alma the Younger

levels.label: C
levels.text: Record-keeping, teaching the people
levels.matchText: Record-keeping, organizing the church

levels.label: D
levels.text: Abinadi's prophecies about Christ

centerText: Abinadi testifies of Christ and is martyred — the prophetic center of the book

title: The Faithful City Become a Harlot
description: Isaiah's lament over Jerusalem's corruption and promise of restoration forms a chiasmus centering on God's purifying judgment.
source: BYU Studies; ScriptureCentral appendix of Isaiah chiasms

levels.label: A
levels.text: The faithful city has become a harlot — full of justice, righteousness lodged in it
levels.matchText: Thou shalt be called the city of righteousness, the faithful city

levels.label: B
levels.text: Thy silver has become dross, thy wine mixed with water
levels.matchText: I will purely purge away thy dross — take away thy tin

levels.label: C
levels.text: Thy princes are rebellious, companions of thieves — everyone loveth bribes
levels.matchText: I will restore thy judges as at first, thy counsellors as at the beginning

levels.label: D
levels.text: The Lord of hosts, the Mighty One of Israel — I will ease me of my adversaries

centerText: The Lord turns his hand against Jerusalem — purifying judgment

title: Discovery at the Judgment Seat
description: The narrative of the discovery of the chief judge's murder is told in chiastic form. This is one of four Book of Mormon chiasms statistically validated as very unlikely to have occurred by chance.
discoveredBy: Boyd Edwards and W. Farrell Edwards (statistical analysis)

levels.label: A
levels.text: Five men ran to the judgment seat
levels.matchText: The five were set at liberty on the morrow

levels.label: B
levels.text: They found the judge fallen to the earth in his blood
levels.matchText: They contended among themselves about the cause of death

levels.label: C
levels.text: The people had not known concerning the multitude / Nephi's prophecy
levels.matchText: The people gathered and saw the five near the dead body

levels.label: D
levels.text: They fell to the earth in astonishment

centerText: They fell to the earth — overwhelming astonishment at fulfilled prophecy
